# pkg.venceslau.dev/ynab

> A complete Go client for the YNAB API v1: all 44 operations behind a
> domain-first surface, exact money arithmetic in Milliunits, first-class
> delta sync, and zero runtime dependencies. YNAB's API says "budget";
> this library says "plan" (YNAB's own product vocabulary) — Plan(id)
> maps to /budgets/{budget_id} on the wire, one-to-one.

Import path: pkg.venceslau.dev/ynab (Go 1.25+). Entry points: ynab.New
(personal access token) or ynab.NewWithTokenSource (OAuth). Everything
plan-scoped hangs off client.Plan(id) — an I/O-free handle with services
Accounts, Categories, Months, Payees, PayeeLocations, MoneyMovements,
Transactions, Scheduled. Money is Milliunits (int64 thousandths, never
floats). Write payloads use the Optional tri-state: unset = omitted,
SetNull = clear server-side, Set = send (zero values included). Delta
sync: ServerKnowledge cursors via Since/TransactionFilter.Since,
SyncState + Plan.Delta + MergeByID. Errors: sentinel taxonomy through
errors.Is (class and sub-code at once, e.g. ErrRateLimited under
ErrTooManyRequests-class); pre-flight *ArgumentError never sends the
request. Rate limit is ~200 requests/hour — prefer delta reads.

## Docs

- [Package documentation](https://pkg.go.dev/pkg.venceslau.dev/ynab): every
  symbol documented, 26 runnable examples
- [README](https://github.com/brunovenceslau/ynab.go/blob/main/README.md):
  install, quick start, the plan handle, delta sync, errors
- [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/brunovenceslau/ynab.go/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md):
  versions and the migration note from the archived v1.x line
- [API_NOTES](https://github.com/brunovenceslau/ynab.go/blob/main/API_NOTES.md):
  observed-vs-documented YNAB API divergences, with live evidence

## Optional

- [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/brunovenceslau/ynab.go/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md):
  gate workflow (lint/test/contract), ground rules, triage SLA
- [SECURITY](https://github.com/brunovenceslau/ynab.go/blob/main/SECURITY.md):
  private vulnerability reporting for a token-handling client
